crm_lead.schema_description: Contains general-purpose tables accessible across the organization.
crm_lead.table_description: Tracks potential sales opportunities (leads) including customer details, sales team assignments, and revenue projections. This table appears to be part of a CRM system, capturing lead information from initial contact through to potential closure or loss.
use_cases: Analyzing sales pipeline by region, salesperson, or product
Tracking lead conversion rates and revenue projections
Identifying high-priority leads for sales teams
Monitoring campaign effectiveness through lead sources
data_quality_notes: The 'created_on' field is stored as TEXT rather than a proper timestamp/datetime type
Several numeric fields are stored as TEXT (expected_revenue) or DOUBLE PRECISION (my_activity_deadline, probability) which may require type conversion for calculations
Many fields are nullable which may impact join operations or data completeness
Country and state fields appear to have inconsistent formatting in sample data

table_columns.name: created_on
table_columns.description: Timestamp of lead creation stored as text (format appears to be YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS)

table_columns.name: opportunity
table_columns.description: Description of the sales opportunity or potential deal

table_columns.name: customer
table_columns.description: Customer name and potentially additional contact information

table_columns.name: contact_name
table_columns.description: Primary contact person for this lead

table_columns.name: email
table_columns.description: Email address of the contact person

table_columns.name: phone
table_columns.description: Phone number of the contact person (may be null)

table_columns.name: city
table_columns.description: City where the customer is located

table_columns.name: state
table_columns.description: State or region where the customer is located (format varies)

table_columns.name: country
table_columns.description: Country where the customer is located

table_columns.name: salesperson
table_columns.description: Name of the salesperson assigned to this lead

table_columns.name: sales_team
table_columns.description: Sales team responsible for this lead

table_columns.name: priority
table_columns.description: Priority level of the lead (e.g., 'Very High')

table_columns.name: activities
table_columns.description: Record of activities related to this lead (may be null)

table_columns.name: responsible_user
table_columns.description: User currently responsible for this lead

table_columns.name: my_activity_deadline
table_columns.description: Deadline for next activity stored as a numeric value (unclear what this represents - may need conversion)

table_columns.name: campaign
table_columns.description: Marketing campaign associated with this lead (may be null)

table_columns.name: medium
table_columns.description: Medium through which the lead was acquired

table_columns.name: source
table_columns.description: Source of the lead

table_columns.name: expected_revenue
table_columns.description: Expected revenue from this lead stored as text (may contain commas and decimal points)

table_columns.name: expected_closing
table_columns.description: Expected closing date for this opportunity (format unclear)

table_columns.name: stage
table_columns.description: Current stage in the sales pipeline

table_columns.name: probability
table_columns.description: Probability of closing this deal (stored as a numeric value between 0-100)

table_columns.name: lost_reason
table_columns.description: Reason why the lead was lost (if applicable)

table_columns.name: tags
table_columns.description: Tags associated with this lead

table_columns.name: properties
table_columns.description: Unclear what this numeric value represents (may need investigation)

metrics.name: Lead Conversion Probability
metrics.definition: The probability of converting a lead into a customer, expressed as a percentage.

metrics.name: Expected Revenue from Leads
metrics.definition: The total expected revenue from all leads, calculated by summing the expected revenue for each lead.
metrics.calculation: SELECT SUM(CAST(REPLACE(expected_revenue, ',', '') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)) FROM crm_lead

business_rules: The 'probability' field in the crm_lead table represents the likelihood of converting a lead into a customer, ranging from 0 to 100.
The 'expected_revenue' field in the crm_lead table is stored as TEXT and may contain commas, requiring conversion to a numeric type for calculations.

common_gotchas.solution: Use REPLACE(expected_revenue, ',', '') to remove commas before casting to a numeric type.
